threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You stop at every bus stop to look at the timetable like it's a magic 8-ball, but locals just know when to hop on.
Caught a night bus last week, two blokes debating the pros and cons of cat cafes—completely serious, like it was a matter of life and death.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a group of kids using the bus stop as a skate ramp while their parents waited for the bus—it felt like home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you find a bar with a swimming pool in the basement. It's a dive for after-hours swims and late-night chats.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know they’re local if they don’t flinch at the BART delays but still complain about how bad the food at La Taqueria has gotten.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Today, a guy in a tuxedo hopped on and started playing the accordion. Best commute accompaniment I’ve had in weeks—only in San Francisco.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think 'tapas' means small plates. Here, we call it 'pica-pica'; locals spread out their meals over hours rather than rushing through.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell the newcomers by their reluctance to use the local language even when everyone else is chattering away in Catalan at the café.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You stand baffled at the bus stop, staring at the timetable like it’s a puzzle, while everyone else knows exactly when the next one will show up.
Saw a guy on the 381 last night arguing with his reflection in the window. Kept swearing at it like it was an ex. Only in Bermondsey.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a couple arguing loudly in Turkish, switch to perfect English mid-fight, then share a smug smile. That's Neukölln for you.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You know those old U-Bahn stations? Some still have their original tiles from the 1920s. They feel like stepping into a time capsule, especially at nights.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know they're local when they can name the taco truck on every corner but can't tell you who runs BART. It's all about priorities.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a guy practicing his stand-up on the 19 Polk today. Only in the Tenderloin would folks be cracking up on a crowded bus at 8 AM.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all the locals flock to Park Güell. Most of us avoid it like the plague—better off at a quiet bar on Verdi.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell they're new when they still look puzzled at the 'bocadillos' pricing at local shops, trying to figure out how a sandwich costs more than a pint.
